images.title: Déchargement d'un goémonier dans le port de Lanildut (Ifremer 00669-78068).jpg
images.description:
The seaweed harvester is a flat-bottomed boat equipped with an articulated mechanical arm that plunges into the water and ends with a scoubidou.

By rotating movements the kelp is torn from the bottom and then stored inside the ship.

Unloading (here at Aber-Ildut, Europe's leading seaweed port) gives rise to a stream of trucks which then go to the Landerneau factory.

images.title: Littoral de Lampaul-Plouarzel (Ifremer 00687-79911 - 32536).jpg
images.description:
Aerial images of the coastline at Lampaul-Plouarzel taken by drone.

The Lampaulais coastline is characterized by a rocky part, topped by low dunes to the north, and by a wider dune field to the south.
